channel.title: Adrian Lissot's Blog
channel.description: Recent content on Adrian Lissot's Blog

item.title: js13k-2020 - 404 BC Pinball
item.description: Check out my participation to js13k 2020, 404 BC Pinball ! Credits: Adrian Lissot, Barthélémy Renucci, Florent Perez, David Sebaoun.

item.title: js13k-2019 - Playback
item.description: Check out my participation to js13k 2019, Playback ! Credits: Adrian Lissot.

item.title: Interface in C#
item.description: C# vs Java interface An interface defines a contract that implementing classes must follow. Java and C# of course have them, but C# gives you more (unnecessary) restrictions about the way you implement interface. Let’s take an example : the Cloneable example. Here’s the corresponding interface, in Java, with its method signature: public interface Cloneable { Cloneable clone(); } In Java, the compiler lets you change the return type of the clone method to some more specific type.

I’m Adrian Lissot, a software engineer and aspiring game developper living in Paris, France. After graduating from IMT Alantique, I decided to join SoftFluent to sharpen my skills in .NET, C#, HTML, CSS and SQL. Even though I mainly code in .NET these days, I’m always open for a new challenge in any language and any paradigm, as long as I’m learning along the way. I currently enjoy the functional languages, such as F# !
